skill.name: discover-skills
skill.description: Find candidate AI skills from SkillRadar by sending a task goal, constraints, keywords, and result limit to a discovery service.
skill.summary: Discovers matching AI skills from an external SkillRadar index.
skill.author: zhiyuzi

security_audit.summary: AI review confirmed network access and filesystem installation guidance, but dismissed weak-crypto, certificate/key, backtick, and entropy alerts as false positives. The remaining issue is high supply-chain risk because remote API results drive ZIP download and installation into AI tool skill directories without checksum, signature, or source verification.

high_findings.title: Remote Skill Installation Without Integrity Verification
high_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to download an install_url ZIP and install it into AI tool skill directories. No checksum, signature, allowlist, or source validation is required before installation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow combines external API results with ZIP installation instructions. The files show no integrity verification step before installing returned skills.

high_findings.title: Automatic Installation Guidance From Remote Recommendations
high_findings.description: The skill says to install based on permissions, or ask only when uncertain or high risk. This can turn an external recommendation into code installation with limited human review.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly connects discovery results to installation behavior. The risk depends on runtime permissions, but the intent is clear.

medium_findings.title: User Task Data Sent to External Discovery API
medium_findings.description: The script sends task_goal, task_constraints, keywords, and max_results to https://api.skillradar.quest. This may expose sensitive project or user intent data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact payload fields and destination host are visible in the script. The data exposure risk is real, though it may be acceptable with user consent.

medium_findings.title: Hidden AI Tool Directory Writes
medium_findings.description: The installation guidance targets hidden project and global skill directories for Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI. These writes can change future tool behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden paths are installation targets, not secret files. They still matter because installed skills can influence later AI tool execution.

medium_findings.title: Shell Example Uses User-Controlled Arguments
medium_findings.description: The documented command passes task text and keywords through a shell command example. Unsafe interpolation by an agent could create command injection risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script itself uses argparse and does not invoke a shell. The risk is in how agents might construct the documented shell command from user text.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Key Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain prose, argparse metadata, candidate descriptions, or a keyword argument name. No weak cryptographic algorithm or certificate file handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto calls, certificate parsing, or key file access at the flagged lines. The alerts appear to match words such as UUID or keywords.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The backtick findings are Markdown inline code and fenced examples. They document paths, URLs, or sample output rather than Ruby command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown, and the flagged backticks appear inside documentation examples. No Ruby code or backtick execution operator is present.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alert Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The entropy alert appears caused by multilingual Markdown and structured examples. No binary, encrypted, or obfuscated payload was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed readable documentation from the first line onward. The scanner reported a heuristic file-level signal, not a specific obfuscated block.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Call
dangerous_patterns.description: The script posts user-provided task details to the SkillRadar discovery endpoint and parses the response as candidate skill data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API base URL, payload, and urlopen call are directly present in the script.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote ZIP Install Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to download, unzip, and install returned skill packages. The instructions do not require verification before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The install_url handling and ZIP installation steps are documented together. No verification control is listed in the workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Tool Configuration Directory Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented installation paths include hidden global and project directories used by AI tools. Modifying them can persist new tool behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The listed directories are explicitly installation destinations. The risk is persistence and future execution influence, not credential theft.

content.value_statement: Teams often do not know which AI skill fits a task. This skill queries SkillRadar and returns ranked candidates with match reasons.
actual_capabilities: Extracts a task goal, optional constraints, keywords, and result count from a user request.
Calls the SkillRadar discovery API with a structured request payload.
Returns ranked candidate skills with names, descriptions, scores, and match reasons.
Shows installation URLs supplied by the discovery service.
Provides guidance for choosing project-level or global skill installation paths.
limitations: Requires network access to the SkillRadar API.
Does not verify downloaded skill ZIP files with checksums or signatures.
Depends on the external index quality and availability.
Returned candidates may not fully match the user's task.

use_cases.title: Find a Skill for a New Task
use_cases.description: Search for candidate skills when the current tool set does not cover a user request.

use_cases.title: Compare Marketplace Candidates
use_cases.description: Review ranked matches and reasons before choosing a skill to inspect or install.

use_cases.title: Route Work to Specialized Tools
use_cases.description: Use task constraints and keywords to identify a more suitable specialist skill.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Skill Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find a skill that can help with summarizing long PDF documents.

prompt_templates.title: Search With Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Task has format and privacy needs
prompt_templates.prompt: Find skills for extracting tables from PDFs. Prefer local processing and CSV output.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Candidate Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for skills that can audit Terraform files, then explain which candidate best matches the task.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Marketplace Triage
prompt_templates.prompt: Find skills for legal document review, list match reasons, and flag candidates that need security review before installation.

output_examples.input: Find a skill for merging several PDF files in batches.
output_examples.output: The skill would return a short ranked list of PDF-related candidates with descriptions, scores, match reasons, and installation links.

output_examples.input: Find a skill that can process private meeting notes without network access.
output_examples.output: The skill may return candidates, but it should highlight the local-processing constraint and warn if candidate details are unclear.

output_examples.input: Find tools for extracting action items from chat logs.
output_examples.output: The skill would search using action-item and chat keywords, then summarize the best matches for user review.

best_practices: Ask for confirmation before installing any returned skill.
Inspect candidate skill files before use, especially scripts and installation steps.
Avoid sending confidential task details to the discovery service.
anti_patterns: Do not auto-install returned ZIP files without integrity checks.
Do not treat high scores as proof that a skill is safe.
Do not send secrets, customer data, or private code details as search text.

faq.question: Does this skill work without internet access?
faq.answer: No. It queries the external SkillRadar API to find candidate skills.

faq.question: Does it install skills automatically?
faq.answer: The documentation describes installation steps, but users should require confirmation and review before installation.

faq.question: Can it verify that a returned skill is safe?
faq.answer: No. It returns candidates and metadata, but it does not audit downloaded packages.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information is sent to SkillRadar?
faq.answer: The script sends the task goal, constraints, keywords, and maximum result count.

faq.question: Why is this skill marked high risk?
faq.answer: It connects remote recommendations to ZIP download and skill installation without built-in integrity verification.
